name: Charneca Circular Route — Aire and Candeeiros Mountains
name_original: Percurso circular da Charneca — Serras de Aire e Candeeiros
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Malhou traverse a landscape characterized by the Aire and Candeeiros Mountains, offering varied terrain for cyclists. The region features significant elevation changes, with routes climbing through hills and valleys. Cyclists can expect to encounter natural springs, bridges, and scenic viewpoints. The area provides a mix of challenging ascents and more moderate paths suitable for different skill levels. Best touring cycling routes around Malhou The most popular touring cycling route is Charneca Circular Route — Aire and Candeeiros Mountains, a 28.6 miles (46.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 25 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial climbs through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Azoia de Cima Viewpoint loop from Arneiro das Milhariças, a difficult 28.0 miles (45.0 km) path. This route offers panoramic views from the Azoia de Cima Viewpoint after significant ascents. Local touring cyclists also love the Ribeira Branca Bridge loop from Zibreira, a 19.7 miles (31.6 km) trail leading through river valleys and past the Ribeira Branca Bridge, often completed in about 1 hour 55 minutes. Touring cycling around Malhou is defined by mountainous terrain, scenic viewpoints, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Malhou are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.5 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 60 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Malhou's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Malhou?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes available around Malhou, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Malhou?
A: The terrain around Malhou is characterized by the Aire and Candeeiros Mountains, featuring significant elevation changes with routes climbing through hills and valleys. You'll encounter a mix of challenging ascents and more moderate paths.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Malhou?
A: Yes, Malhou offers a few easier touring cycling routes. While many routes involve significant climbs, there are options designed for those new to touring cycling or looking for a less strenuous ride.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Malhou?
A: Experienced cyclists looking for a challenge might enjoy the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21950525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moitas Venda – Peão Fountain - Monsanto loop from Alcanena&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult route covers over 48 miles (78 km) with more than 3,300 feet (1,000 meters) of elevation gain, offering a demanding ride through the mountainous landscape.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Malhou?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes in Malhou are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32189179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peão Fountain - Monsanto – Campsite in Olive Grove loop from Zibreira&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 17-mile (27.6 km) loop that allows you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Malhou?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Malhou is typically during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these months, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, wetter conditions of winter.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while cycling around Malhou?
A: While cycling around Malhou, you can explore several natural attractions. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5593694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peão Fountain - Monsanto&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5354903" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Alviela River (Olhos de Água)&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5597739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcanena MTB Center – Alviela Spring&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also pass by scenic viewpoints and natural springs.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Malhou?
A: The komoot community rates the touring cycling routes in Malhou highly, with an average score of 3.5 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied mountainous terrain, the scenic viewpoints, and the challenging ascents that provide rewarding panoramic views.

Q: Are there any routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Yes, several routes offer panoramic views, especially those that climb higher into the Aire and Candeeiros Mountains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32157755" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azoia de Cima Viewpoint loop from Arneiro das Milhariças&lt;/a&gt; is specifically known for its panoramic vistas after significant ascents.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that follow river valleys?
A: Yes, you can find routes that lead through river valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21948532" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeira Branca Bridge loop from Zibreira&lt;/a&gt; is a notable example, taking you through river valleys and past the historic Ribeira Branca Bridge.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on touring cycling routes in Malhou?
A: Given the mountainous landscape of the Aire and Candeeiros Mountains, touring cycling routes in Malhou often feature significant elevation gain. Even moderate routes can have several hundred meters of ascent, while difficult routes can exceed 1,000 meters (3,300 feet) of climbing.

Q: Are there any specific bridges or historical points of interest along the routes?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate interesting bridges and historical points. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21948532" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeira Branca Bridge loop from Zibreira&lt;/a&gt; passes by the Ribeira Branca Bridge. Additionally, you might encounter other historical structures or natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5593543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte da Ferreira&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: The Fonte do Peão, built in 1884, is one of the symbols of the parish of Monsanto. It is covered in tiles that depict rural life. The water in the Fonte do Peão comes from a spring and flows regularly throughout the year. According to information obtained from local residents, the water is good for human consumption and is used as a source of water for the many travellers who pass through there.
